Penn State University · COMM 467 · Bellisario College of Communications
The 467 Chronicle
State College, Pa.Story Budget Exercise

Your Assignment

  1. Read each story carefully. Skim once you have a solid grasp of it.
  2. Rank all nine stories 1–9 by newsworthiness (1 = most urgent/important) and briefly explain each ranking decision.
  3. For each story, write a headline (~60 chars, title case) and a dek (~60 chars, sentence style) in the fields between the photo and story text.
  4. Select 5 stories whose photos run on the homepage. Then choose one for the top news lead (most important breaking story) and one for the feature spot (the human-interest story with the big photo).
  5. Enter your name above, then click Download to submit via Canvas.
Step 1 — Rank All Nine Stories by Newsworthiness
Step 2 — Homepage Decisions

Which 5 stories get photos?

The homepage has exactly 5 photo slots. Check five.

0 of 5 selected

Top news lead

The most urgent breaking story — goes at top of page, drives the homepage.

Feature spot

A human-interest story given a big photo and prominent display — often not the hardest news.
